findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 28. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Repeated page header/footer content in HTML
findings.evidence: The following text blocks repeat 3+ times, likely page headers/footers that should not appear in the body: 'where,' (3x).

findings.description: Italic emphasis removed from quoted Consent Order regulatory text in Section 2.3
findings.evidence: PDF pages 9-10 show the Consent Order quoted text (starting with 'c. Outfall 002 Trendline: i. Starting no later than August 31, 2020, Chemours shall...') rendered entirely in italics to distinguish it from the document's own narrative. In the HTML (chunk 10), this same quoted text is rendered in regular (non-italic) style, losing the visual distinction between regulatory quoted language and the document's own prose.

findings.description: In Equations 2 and 3, the PDF distinguishes between uppercase C (total PFAS concentration) and lowercase c (individual compound concentration). The HTML converts all instances to uppercase C, losing this mathematical distinction.
findings.evidence: PDF Eq 2 second line: '= ∑ c_OF002,j,i × Q_OF002,j × F' (lowercase c). HTML: '= ∑ C_OF002,j,i × Q_OF002,j × F' (uppercase C). Same issue in variable definitions: PDF 'c_OF002,j,i = is the measured or estimated concentration of the ith PFAS compound' vs HTML 'C_OF002,j,i = is the measured or estimated concentration of the ith PFAS compound'. The same discrepancy occurs in Equation 3 with c_intake,k,i changed to C_intake,k,i.

findings.description: Figure 3 (Flowchart for Determining the Concentration for Loading at the River Water Intake) from PDF page 17 is missing its own tag in the HTML. Only a text description of the flowchart is present.
findings.evidence: PDF page 17 contains a separate flowchart graphic for Figure 3. The HTML has an tag only for Figure 2 (alt='Flowchart for Determining the Concentration for Loading at Outfall 002') but no corresponding tag for Figure 3. The Figure 3 flowchart logic is rendered as plain text only: 'Was a 24-hr composite sample collected? YES → Use 24-hr composite sample concentration... NO ↓ Use 60-day rolling average dry concentration'.
